references.helpText: Add references to web pages or documents that provide more context about the detected threat.
references.guidanceLinksOnly: Use links to web pages or documents only.
references.guidanceNoRawContent: Do not link to EVTX files, PCAPs, or other raw content.
references.guidanceNoMitreLinks: Do not include links to MITRE ATT&CK techniques (we use tags for that).
references.examplesCaption: Examples: blog posts, tweets, project pages, manual pages, advisories, discussions.
references.label: Reference
references.placeholder: Enter reference and press Enter or click Add

tags.helpText: Use tags from MITRE ATT&CK, CAR and tags for CVE numbers.
tags.examplesCaption: Examples tags: attack.credential_access, attack.t1003.002, car.2013-07-001, cve.2020.10189
tags.formatRules: Use lower-case tags only. Use . or - as divider in tag names. Replace space with an underscore _.
tags.addPlaceholder: Enter tag and press Enter or click Add

logSource.helpText: Application and the type that is required in the detection. It consists of three attributes that are evaluated automatically by the converters and an arbitrary number of optional elements. We recommend using a "definition" value in cases in which further explanation is necessary.
logSource.definitionHelper: Describe the log source, including log verbosity level or configurations.

detection.helpNoSingleList: If your list consists of a single element, don't use a list.
detection.helpComments: Put comments on lines if you like to (use 2 spaces to separate the expression from your comment, e.g. - 'cmd.exe' # command line).
detection.helpNoRegex: Don't use regular expressions unless you really have to (e.g. instead of CommandLine|re: '\\payload.*\skeyset' use CommandLine|contains|all with the values \payload and keyset).
detection.helpFieldNames: In new sources use the field names as they appear in the log source, remove spaces and keep hyphens (e.g. SAM User Account becomes SAMUserAccount).
detection.helpNoSiemLogic: Don't use SIEM specific logic in your condition.

fields.helpText: These are the fields that are very helpful in the evaluation of a certain event. For example, it is helpful to know the parent process of a process that contains suspicious strings in its command line parameters. These fields could be extracted automatically and presented to the analyst in order to speed up the analysis.

falsePositives.helpText: Think about possible false positive conditions that could also trigger the rule. This list should contain useful hints for an analyst. E.g. the comment "Legitimate processes that delete the shadow copies" can be a hint for an analyst to check for backup processes on that system or ask for any unusual administrative activity that involved the deletion of the local volume shadow copies.

keywordsInput.label: Keyword
keywordsInput.placeholder: Enter keyword and press Enter or click Add

selectionConditions.valuePlaceholder: Enter value and press Enter or click Add
selectionConditions.requiredAlert: Field and Value are required for a condition.

tagsForm.addPlaceholder: Enter tag and press Enter or click Add
tagsForm.helpText: Tags help categorize and identify rules. Common tags include malware family names, attack techniques, or file types.

conditionsForm.helpText: Conditions define when the rule should match. String match conditions require at least one string to be defined.

ruleOptions.messagePlaceholder: Brief description of what this rule detects

basicMetadataSection.requiredAlert: Both metadata key and value are required.
